channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Whale disposal scrutinised after boat ramp 'debacle' attracts sharks
item.description: Marine scientists want to see whale carcasses towed offshore and disposed of out to sea in a process they say is safer, cheaper and more ecologically friendly.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt

media:group.media:description: The operation to remove the whale left the water around the Bellambi Boat Ramp churned and bloodied.

item.title: The invisible problem sitting under Queensland streets and homes
item.description: At least 22,000km of water mains in Queensland's network are headed towards the end of their useful life span, leading to an "infrastructure cliff".
item.dc:creator: Hannah Moon

media:group.media:description: Kristy Reid-Smith says there have been multiple rounds of clean-up after their home flooded.

item.title: Leah Purcell's The Drover's Wife becomes a powerful new opera
item.description: Leah Purcell’s The Drover’s Wife is being transformed into an opera with music by George Palmer and starring soprano Nina Korbe.
item.dc:creator: Ria Andriani

media:group.media:description: Nina Korbe plays Molly Johnson in the titular Drover's Wife, taking over the role from Leah Purcell.

item.title: How Nigel Farage went from outsider to contender for UK PM
item.description: Nigel Farage has his eye on the prime minister's job himself and, if Reform can sustain this level of support at a general election, the keys to Downing Street may fall to him.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Wearmouth

media:group.media:description: Reform UK's Nigel Farage has triumphed in the UK local elections, in results that put him on course to be the country's next prime minister.

item.title: MP threatens to challenge UK PM Starmer after local election defeat
item.description: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is facing a new setback to his leadership after a former minister said she would challenge him for the top job.

media:group.media:description: Following massive losses in the UK's local elections, several prominent voices in Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's Labour party have called for him to step down.

item.title: Breeding program gives endangered frog with 'baritone' call a fighting chance
item.description: Scientists hope the successful captive breeding and release of the endangered Watson's tree frog in Victoria's far east will help aid the species' survival, after its habitat was decimated during the Black Summer bushfires.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope

media:group.media:description: The Watson's tree frog was feared extinct after there were no sightings in the wild for 19 years.

item.title: Women's players sick of being told 'don't bite the hand that feeds you'
item.description: Players from various codes across the country will protest gender-based violence and call for greater accountability from clubs when instances of mistreatment arise.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall

media:group.media:description: Jaydan Thomson left her former club after filing a court protection order against a male player.

item.title: Who is responsible for the problems in Alice Springs town camps?
item.description: Conditions in town camps in Alice Springs are under intense scrutiny, but the residents are caught in a tangled web of bureaucracy with little transparency on the money being spent and who exactly is responsible for what.
item.dc:creator: Dana Morse

media:group.media:description: Harley wants to stay in the community he grew up in and help to improve it for his sons.

item.title: Australian cafe owner looking to protect signature drink from imitators
item.description: Melbourne cafe owner Caleb Cha is looking into how he can legally stop shops in Indonesia from imitating his signature Tiger Bomb coffee.
item.dc:creator: Natasya Salim and Raffa Athallah

media:group.media:description: Melbourne cafe owner Caleb Cha says imitators of his signature Tiger Bomb coffee have prompted him to consider what he can do to protect it.

item.title: 'Easy to get influenced': What cheaper weight-loss drugs means for millions
item.description: India has become one of the first countries to introduce cheaper, generic brands of weight loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy, but health professionals warn a lack of regulation and oversight is causing problems.
item.dc:creator: Claire Campbell

media:group.media:description: Pankhuri Pareek is one of an increasing number of Indian women prescribed Mounjaro to lose weight.

item.title: The 'magnificent' mansion that housed movie stars, hippies and refugees
item.description: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Bonica

media:group.media:description: Labassa today sits out of view in a quiet cul-de-sac.

item.title: How everyone is making money — and what it means for the tax they pay
item.description: In Australia, the way people earn their money affects the rate of tax they pay — and top earners have a very different income mix from everyone else.
item.dc:creator: Andie Noonan, Margaret Burin, Simon Elvery, Jarrod Fankhauser, Jessica Riga, and Matt Liddy

item.title: Ancient drought resistant winegrape instilling hope for struggling farmers
item.description: In an Australian first, two South Australian winegrape growers are trialling an ancient variety from Cyprus that is reducing water consumption and reconnecting them to their Mediterranean roots.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Walters

media:group.media:description: Dr Copper says Xynisteri is "not the answer to everyone's prayers but it's part of a solution".

item.title: Farrer result turns One Nation from protest to genuine electoral threat
item.description: The result in Farrer has made One Nation’s attempts to become the natural home for conservative Australians look less like a protest and more like a genuine electoral threat.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: One Nation Leader Pauline Hanson addressed a jubilant victory party, telling supporters "we're coming after those other seats".

item.title: John Polson was headed for jail. One phone call changed his life forever
item.description: John Polson was a troubled teen when a sliding doors moment led him into the world of acting. He went on to create Tropfest, one of the world's biggest short film festivals, changing his life and the Australian industry.
item.dc:creator: Leisa Scott and Sarah Grant

media:group.media:description: John Polson takes the stage at the 2003 Tropfest alongside actor Russell Crowe.

item.title: The RBA's post-COVID rate hikes caused Australians to work more
item.description: The International Monetary Fund found that thousands of Australians took on a second or third job when the RBA hiked rates rapidly in 2022 and 2023. It expected the opposite.
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Tighter monetary policy is supposed to dampen aggregate demand and discourage employers from hiring workers.

item.title: Councils to get federal funds for roads, pipes and wires in housing push
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers will reveal details of the $2 billion fund in Tuesday's federal budget, declaring the cash injection will add an extra 65,000 homes over a decade.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: This week's budget will include $2 billion for councils and state utility companies to deliver utilities and road infrastructure.

item.title: We asked the IRGC for their Strait of Hormuz rules and they emailed us back
item.description: Amid a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, the IRGC has launched a maritime authority for the Strait of Hormuz. The Persian Gulf Strait Authority says ships need to email their application for passage. So, we sent them a request.
item.dc:creator: Emily Clark and Tavleen Singh

media:group.media:description: The IRGC has asked ships to submit application forms to request passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: New exhibition charts motherhood from the 15th century to today
item.description: Mother at Melbourne's NGV offers contrasting depictions of motherhood, from Toscani's Madonna and Child to First Nations weaving.
item.dc:creator: Bhakthi Puvanenthiran

media:group.media:description: The NGV's exhibition Mother draws extensively on works from existing collections, as it celebrates and explores what it means to be a mother.

item.title: Step away from the spellcheck. It’s quiz time
item.description: Ready to hone your homophones and verbalise your vowels? Let's get spelling!

media:group.media:description: Guy Montgomery on the set of Guy Mont-Spelling Bee for season three.

item.title: We're living in the age of exhaustion. Who pays the price of burnout?
item.description: Burnout is everywhere — in resignation letters, therapy sessions and late-night emails. Yet the debate over what it is reveals a bigger question: Should workplaces bear responsibility for the damage modern work can cause?
item.dc:creator: Brigid Delaney

item.title: Protests, nudity and prime ministerial heat: 25 years of Big Brother
item.description: Imported to Australia 25 years ago, Big Brother introduced the country to the concept of 24-hour monitoring as entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Velvet Winter

media:group.media:description: The cast of the first season of Big Brother Australia before going into the house in 2001.

item.title: Liberal vote plummets as One Nation scores historic win in Farrer
item.description: One Nation scores an historic win in the Farrer by-election, marking the first time the party has won a Lower House seat in federal parliament.
item.dc:creator: Bruce MacKenzie

media:group.media:description: One Nation candidate David Farley (left) and party leader Pauline Hanson (right) celebrate victory in the Farrer by-election.

item.title: Putin attends scaled-back Victory Day parade amid Ukraine worries
item.description: Once used to show off Russia's vast military, including its nuclear-capable missiles, this year's Victory Day ​parade had no tanks or other military equipment ‌rolling over the cobblestones of Red Square.

media:group.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin delivers a speech during Victory Day celebrations at Red Square.

item.title: Melbourne City surges into ALW grand final after young gun's stunning strike
item.description: Shelby McMahon fires Melbourne City into the A-League Women's grand final, with her side downing Melbourne Victory 2-0 on aggregate.

media:group.media:description: McMahon's stunning strike fired City into the A-League Women's grand final.
